verdict.answer_first: A states the core rule immediately: "never change the meaning of an existing contract; only add to it"; B starts with framing, then gives the principle in sentence 2: "most changes shouldn't require a new version at all"
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleanly to one default: "default to URL path versioning (`/v1/resource` → `/v2/resource`)"; B recommends path versioning but spends more space on alternatives like "date-based versions"
verdict.fabrication: B invents/uses local context the asker never gave: "This dir (`/private/tmp`) isn't a repo"
verdict.actionability: B is slightly easier to operationalize right now because it adds concrete defaults and enforcement tools: "OpenAPI spec + CI breaking-change linter (e.g. `oasdiff`, Optic, or Spectral)"
